Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. To determine how many bottles she needs for a leap year, we first calculate the total number of days in a leap year, which is 366 days. Since she uses shampoo every other day, she will use it 366 / 2 = 183 times. Each bottle contains 14 ounces of shampoo, and she uses 1/4 ounce per use, so the total amount of shampoo needed is 183 * 1/4 = 45.75 ounces. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she will need to round up to the next whole number, which is 46 bottles. [total_shampoo_needed] is the total amount of shampoo required for the leap year. [number_of_bottles] is the number of bottles Brittany needs to buy.

Brittany needs to buy [number_of_bottles] bottles of shampoo to cover her needs for the leap year. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, and she uses 1/4 ounce every other day. The total amount of shampoo needed is [total_shampoo_needed] ounces. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she will need to round up to the next whole number, which is [number_of_bottles].
